meta_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between vision and revenue. Learn to use Git, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build a high-converting MVP portfolio that lands your first customers.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for a portfolio to appear. Use AI-driven Git workflows to rapidly prototype, document, and deploy production-grade SaaS components. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for iterative development, you transform raw ideas into a verifiable, high-value product portfolio that proves your technical competence to investors and clients.
content.intro:
The blank screen is a founder's silent adversary. You have the vision, the market insight, and the drive, but you lack the one thing the market demands: proof of work. In the hyper-competitive SaaS landscape, a empty GitHub profile is a liability that keeps your runway short and your credibility low. You don't need years of legacy code; you need high-impact velocity.

We are shifting the paradigm from 'coding from scratch' to 'orchestrating with AI.' By integrating Antigravity for structural precision and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can turn a weekend of focused effort into a portfolio that commands authority. This isn't just about writing functions; it's about building a narrative of technical execution that converts visitors into stakeholders.

steps.title: Define the MVP Business Logic
steps.description: Before touching Git, define the core value proposition. Use Antigravity to map out your database schema and API endpoints to ensure your code solves a specific, monetizable problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Architect a lean SaaS backend for a [Product Idea]. Focus on authentication, Stripe subscription integration, and a CRUD interface for user-generated content. Output the database schema and API routing structure.

steps.title: Initialize Git with Strategic Branching
steps.description: Your Git history is your portfolio. Use feature-based branching to show potential clients how you think, debug, and iterate over time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional gitignore for a modern Next.js/Node stack and define a branching strategy that emphasizes iterative feature development.

steps.title: Automated Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write clean, modular code. The key is to generate readable, well-commented functions that demonstrate high-level engineering patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure, robust Next.js API route for handling user payments. Include input validation using Zod and error handling that follows production best practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement stripe checkout flow with robust error handling'

steps.title: Documentation as a Competitive Advantage
steps.description: A portfolio without READMEs is invisible. Use AI to document your architecture, explaining 'why' you chose specific tools to demonstrate seniority.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a compelling README.md for this SaaS project. Include a 'Why this architecture' section, setup instructions, and a clear overview of the business problem solved.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a high-quality technical README.md based on the current codebase'

steps.title: Atomic Commits for Portfolio Visibility
steps.description: Clean, atomic commits prove you understand professional workflow. Use Claude to write commit messages that describe the business impact of each change.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the changes made in the last session into a professional, conventional commit message that highlights the feature added and the technical approach taken.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'feat: implement secure payment processing with Stripe SDK'

pro_tips: Treat your commit history like a technical blog; describe the business logic, not just the code changes.
Use Claude Code to create 'refactor' sessions that clean up technical debt, demonstrating long-term maintenance skills.
Keep your README files focused on the business problem solved, not just the tech stack used.
Tag your releases in Git to show you understand product versioning and milestone delivery.
Link your live demo to your GitHub repository via a prominent banner in your README to maximize conversion.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to a platform like Vercel or Railway, add your live URL to your GitHub profile, and start sharing your build-in-public journey on Twitter/LinkedIn.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting a lack of history hold you back from shipping the next big thing. By leveraging these tools, you build faster, document smarter, and gain the credibility needed to secure your first paying customer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
